chapter: Chapter 8: Nature of Matter – Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

questions.question: What is matter?
questions.answer: Matter is anything that has mass and occupies space.

questions.question: What is a mixture?
questions.answer: A mixture is a combination of two or more substances that are not chemically combined.

questions.question: What are the components of a mixture?
questions.answer: The individual substances present in a mixture are called its components.

questions.question: What is a uniform mixture?
questions.answer: A uniform mixture is a mixture in which the components are evenly distributed.

questions.question: Give one example of a non-uniform mixture.
questions.answer: Sand mixed with water is an example of a non-uniform mixture.

questions.question: Why is air considered a mixture?
questions.answer: Air is a mixture because it contains different gases like nitrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: What happens when carbon dioxide reacts with limewater?
questions.answer: Limewater turns milky due to the formation of calcium carbonate.

questions.question: What is a pure substance?
questions.answer: A pure substance is made up of only one type of particle.

questions.question: What is an element?
questions.answer: An element is a pure substance made of only one type of atom.

questions.question: What is an atom?
questions.answer: An atom is the smallest particle of an element that retains its properties.

questions.question: What is a molecule?
questions.answer: A molecule is formed when two or more atoms combine together.

questions.question: What is electrolysis?
questions.answer: Electrolysis is the process of using electricity to break down a compound into simpler substances.

questions.question: Which gases are produced during electrolysis of water?
questions.answer: Hydrogen and oxygen gases are produced.

questions.question: What is a compound?
questions.answer: A compound is a substance formed when two or more elements combine chemically.

questions.question: Give one example of a compound.
questions.answer: Water is an example of a compound.

questions.question: Why are the properties of compounds different from their elements?
questions.answer: Because elements chemically combine to form new substances with different properties.

questions.question: What happens when iron and sulfur are heated together?
questions.answer: They react chemically to form iron sulfide.

questions.question: What is an alloy?
questions.answer: An alloy is a mixture of two or more metals.

questions.question: Give one example of an alloy.
questions.answer: Bronze is an alloy made of copper and tin.

questions.question: What are minerals?
questions.answer: Minerals are naturally occurring solid substances found in the Earth.

questions.question: Give one example of a mineral.
questions.answer: Quartz is an example of a mineral.

questions.question: Why are minerals important?
questions.answer: Minerals are used to make many useful materials like metals, cement, and glass.

questions.question: How can mixtures be separated?
questions.answer: Mixtures can be separated using physical methods such as filtration, evaporation, or magnetic separation.

questions.question: Give one example of separation of a mixture.
questions.answer: Iron filings can be separated from sand using a magnet.

questions.question: Why is the study of matter important?
questions.answer: The study of matter helps scientists understand materials and develop useful products.
